title: Nowruz Address to the Iranian Nation

paragraphs.text: In the name of God, the Most Compassionate, the Most Merciful. I congratulate the great Iranian nation, all Iranians across the world, and all nations that celebrate Nowruz on the arrival of the new year. I pray to God Almighty to make this year a year of blessing, honor, and progress for our dear nation.

I have designated this new year as the year of the "Jump in Production with the People's Participation." The emphasis on production is a continuation of our national priorities. But this year I want to stress the role of the people. The government alone cannot transform the economy. It is the creativity, the entrepreneurship, and the hard work of the Iranian people that will drive our national production forward.

The enemies of this nation have spent decades trying to bring Iran to its knees through sanctions and economic pressure. They imagined that they could force the Iranian people to surrender by making their daily lives difficult. But they miscalculated. The Iranian nation has shown, year after year, that it will not abandon its principles under pressure. Our people have endured hardship with dignity, and our economy, despite all the obstacles, has continued to grow.

Let me share some facts. Despite the most severe sanctions regime in modern history, Iran’s GDP grew last year. Our non-oil exports reached record levels. Our knowledge-based companies have expanded to more than eight thousand enterprises, generating significant employment and revenue. This is the result of sanctions being turned into opportunities. When they cut us off from imported goods, our engineers and entrepreneurs found ways to produce them domestically.

I do not deny that our people face economic difficulties. Inflation remains high, and many families struggle with the rising cost of living. I am aware of these hardships, and they weigh heavily on me. The government must do more to control inflation, to support vulnerable households, and to ensure that the fruits of economic growth are distributed fairly. Economic justice is not merely an administrative matter. It is a religious and moral obligation.

The issue of Palestine has entered a new and deeply painful chapter. The atrocities committed against the people of Gaza are a stain on the conscience of humanity. Tens of thousands of civilians, the majority of them women and children, have been killed in what can only be described as a campaign of collective punishment. The world watches, and the international institutions that claim to uphold human rights have proven impotent in the face of this catastrophe.

Iran has always stood with the Palestinian people, and we will continue to do so. This is not a sectarian issue. This is a matter of fundamental justice. The Palestinian people have the right to self-determination, the right to return to their homes, and the right to live in dignity and freedom. No amount of military force can extinguish a people’s desire for liberation. History has shown this truth again and again.

The Western powers speak of rules and order, but they apply these rules selectively. When their allies violate international law, there is silence. When their interests are at stake, they bypass the very institutions they created. This hypocrisy is not lost on the nations of the world. The era of Western hegemony is drawing to a close, and a new international order is emerging — one based on multipolarity, mutual respect, and genuine sovereignty.

Iran’s defense capabilities are not negotiable. Our missile program, our defense industries, and our strategic deterrence exist solely to protect our nation from aggression. We have seen what happens to countries that disarm under Western pressure. We have witnessed the fate of nations that trusted Western guarantees. Iran will never put itself in a position of vulnerability. Our strength is the guarantor of our independence.

I want to address our young people directly. You are the greatest asset of this nation. Your talent, your energy, your creativity are the foundation upon which Iran’s future will be built. I know that many of you face challenges — in finding employment, in realizing your aspirations. I urge the government to remove the obstacles that prevent our young people from contributing to national progress. And I urge our youth to have confidence in their own abilities and in their country’s future.

Science and technology are the pillars of national power in the twenty-first century. Iran has made remarkable progress in fields ranging from nuclear science to nanotechnology, from biotechnology to aerospace engineering. Our universities produce world-class researchers. Our scientists have achieved breakthroughs despite being cut off from international collaboration by unjust sanctions. This scientific progress must continue and accelerate.

The Islamic Republic has survived forty-five years of hostility from the most powerful nations on earth. We have survived war, sanctions, assassination of our scientists, and constant political pressure. And we have not merely survived — we have grown stronger. This resilience is rooted in our faith, our culture, and the revolutionary spirit of our people. It cannot be broken by external pressure.

Our relations with our neighbors are of paramount importance. Iran seeks peace and cooperation with all nations in the region. We have demonstrated this through diplomatic initiatives and through our willingness to engage in dialogue. The security of the Persian Gulf, of West Asia, must be ensured by the nations of the region themselves, not by foreign powers stationed thousands of kilometers away.

I call upon government officials at all levels to serve the people with humility and efficiency. Public service is a sacred trust. Those who hold positions of responsibility must remember that they are servants of the people, not their masters. Corruption, waste, and bureaucratic indifference are betrayals of this trust. The people have the right to demand accountability, and officials have the duty to provide it.

The elections held last year demonstrated the vitality of our political system. Despite the wishes of our enemies, the Iranian people came out and voted. Every election in the Islamic Republic is an expression of national sovereignty and popular will. I encourage all eligible citizens to participate in future elections with enthusiasm and a sense of responsibility. Your vote is your voice in shaping the destiny of this nation.

As we enter this new year, let us renew our commitment to the values that have sustained us. Faith, unity, self-reliance, and resistance against oppression — these are not slogans. They are the lived experience of the Iranian nation over four and a half decades. With trust in God and confidence in our people, we will overcome every challenge. I wish all Iranians a blessed and prosperous new year.
